Title: PROTEIN TARGETING TO STARCH Is Required for Localising GRANULE-BOUND STARCH SYNTHASE to Starch Granules and for Normal Amylose Synthesis in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in Arabidopsis leaves; in tobacco transient assays PTST-YFP localizes in plastid stroma
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major determinant of starch physicochemical properties relevant to food quality and non-food industrial uses
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plastidial protein with N-terminal coiled-coil domain and C-terminal CBM48 carbohydrate-binding module
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Primarily chloroplast/plastid stroma; transient/low abundance on starch granules
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutants; amylose quantification by iodine colorimetry and Sepharose CL-2B chromatography; granule-bound protein immunoblotting; soluble/insoluble fractionation; in vitro starch-binding assay; transient expression/confocal localization in tobacco; GBSS overexpression tests; in vitro pull-down and in planta co-immunoprecipitation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PTST is specifically required for normal amylose synthesis by targeting GBSS to starch granules; loss of PTST causes amylose-free starch without changing total starch content
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PTST knockout mutants (ptst-1 and ptst-2) synthesize amylose-free starch while total starch content remains near wild type. 2. Loss of PTST causes a dramatic (~100-fold) reduction of granule-bound GBSS protein without changing GBSS transcript abundance, indicating PTST acts post-transcriptionally in GBSS localization. 3. PTST physically interacts with GBSS via coiled-coil regions and requires its CBM48 domain to bind starch; this starch-binding function is necessary for GBSS targeting to granules in vivo.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PTST is highly conserved in green plants and present as a single-copy gene in Arabidopsis. W217A/W255A mutations in the CBM48 abolish starch binding and prevent GBSS granule localization. In ptst sex4 double mutants, some amylose accumulates slowly over prolonged starch retention, indicating PTST greatly enhances targeting efficiency rather than being absolutely required for any GBSS-starch contact.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising biotechnology target to reduce amylose specifically with little effect on total starch; PTST overexpression, potentially together with GBSS, may increase amylose while avoiding yield penalties associated with branching-enzyme suppression.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No total starch penalty detected in Arabidopsis leaves; effectiveness in crop storage organs may vary if partial redundancy or prolonged starch synthesis permits some amylose accumulation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Phylogenetic analysis from NCBI/Phytozome sequences; TreeBASE accession study 16699; rice organ expression from Genevestigator Rice Genome 51K array (S8 Fig.); qPCR of GBSS transcripts in ptst (S2 Data)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12-h light/12-h dark growth regime; leaf age influences amylose accumulation in ptst sex4 because prolonged starch retention permits some residual GBSS action
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for GBSS-GFP overexpression and transient co-expression assays in tobacco
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in Arabidopsis leaves; rice PTST expression noted in both leaf and developing endosperm from Genevestigator

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf; transcript level peaks 2 h into the light period
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Amylose synthesis branch of starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal polymer elongation step within starch granule
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to elongate essentially linear α-1,4-glucan chains embedded in starch granules, thereby synthesizing amylose
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major determinant of starch physicochemical behavior important for cooking quality and food/non-food industrial processing
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycosyltransferase family starch synthase; predicted C-terminal coiled-coil helix
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast starch granule; plastid stroma when not efficiently targeted
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gbss mutant analysis; starch granule-bound protein immunoblotting; transient co-expression localization in tobacco; GBSS-GFP overexpression in Arabidopsis; in vitro pull-down assay; in planta co-immunoprecipitation; in-gel activity assay; metabolite profiling of amylose
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; paper states GBSS previously appears not to catalyse the rate-limiting step in amylose synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for amylose synthesis; gbss mutants produce amylose-free starch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GBSS is the key enzyme specifically responsible for amylose synthesis in Arabidopsis leaf starch. 2. GBSS abundance on starch granules is reduced about 100-fold in ptst mutants despite unchanged GBSS transcript levels, indicating defective post-transcriptional targeting/localization. 3. GBSS directly interacts with PTST through a predicted coiled-coil helix, and this interaction is required for efficient localization of GBSS to starch granules and normal amylose accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis GBSS did not localize to starch granules in tobacco unless Arabidopsis PTST was co-expressed; unbound GBSS in ptst is inferred to be unstable/degraded. Mutations K441E and E452K strongly disrupt PTST interaction; E486A abolishes catalytic activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GBSS is a direct amylose-control gene, but prior overexpression in potato and wheat did not increase amylose; stacking with PTST overexpression may be more effective for amylose enhancement.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression alone may not increase amylose; free unbound GBSS is unstable.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qPCR of GBSS transcripts in ptst (S2 Data); no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12-h light/12-h dark cycle; analyses in leaf starch at end of day/night
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for GBSS-GFP overexpression in Arabidopsis and transient expression constructs in tobacco
